data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <bead id="ddx-ea76e1b8">
    <title>checks: ship Go production-reachability check (library/checks/go-production-reachability)</title>
    <description>
Ship the Go-specific production-reachability check as a library resource conforming to the REACH-PROTO check protocol. NOT installed by default for arbitrary projects (Go-opinion would leak through ddx init). This project (ddx itself) opts in by adding .ddx/checks/production-reachability.yaml.

DESIGN
- Location: library/checks/go-production-reachability/ (alongside library/personas/, library/patterns/)
- Implementation: a small Go binary or shell script invoking golang.org/x/tools/cmd/deadcode rooted at configurable entry packages.
- Default entry roots for ddx self-use: cli/cmd/..., cli/internal/server/graphql/..., cli/internal/server/http/...
- Logic:
  1. Read DIFF_BASE..DIFF_HEAD from env. Compute set of new Go symbols (functions, methods, types) added by the diff via AST parse of changed files.
  2. Run deadcode (or callgraph) rooted at the configured entry packages, EXCLUDING _test.go from roots and from reachability.
  3. For each new symbol not reachable from any entry root → violation { file, line, symbol, kind: "unreachable", detail: "no path from entry roots: <list>" }
  4. Recognize // wiring:pending <bead-id> annotation as escape hatch for individual symbols. The check verifies the named bead exists and is open via local beads.jsonl read; otherwise the annotation is rejected.
  5. Write ${EVIDENCE_DIR}/production-reachability.json with the protocol-defined schema.
- Config: library/checks/go-production-reachability/check.yaml template that operators copy to .ddx/checks/ and customize entry_roots for their project.

DOGFOOD
- This project installs it: write .ddx/checks/production-reachability.yaml referencing the library check + cli's entry roots.
- The next ddx try on any bead touching cli/**/*.go will exercise the check.

NOT IN SCOPE
- Auto-install during ddx init (would leak Go-opinion). Operators opt in manually or via ddx install check (future, separate bead).
- Coverage-based "exercised via CLI" check (entered_via flavor). Static reachability is sufficient for the immediate problem.
- Python/TS/Rust analogues. Future contributions; protocol supports them.

DEP: REACH-PROTO (above)
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. library/checks/go-production-reachability/ exists with: README.md (usage), check.yaml (template), implementation (binary or script).
2. Implementation reads DIFF_BASE/DIFF_HEAD/PROJECT_ROOT/EVIDENCE_DIR from env; computes new symbols from diff; runs deadcode-rooted reachability; writes result.json conforming to REACH-PROTO schema.
3. // wiring:pending <bead-id> annotation parsed; bead existence + open-status verified against .ddx/beads.jsonl; invalid annotation = violation.
4. _test.go files excluded from both root selection and reachability calculation (so a unit-test-only reference does NOT satisfy the check).
5. Self-test: synthetic project with one unwired exported function returns status=block with that symbol in violations.
6. Self-test: same project with the function wired into cmd handler returns status=pass.
7. Self-test: same project with // wiring:pending annotation citing an open bead returns status=pass; annotation citing a closed/missing bead returns status=block.
8. This project installs it: .ddx/checks/production-reachability.yaml committed; ddx ac run on any current bead succeeds (or BLOCKs with the known unwired functions — see REACH-BACKFILL for resolution).
9. cd cli && go test green; lefthook pre-commit passes.
10. Evidence file at .ddx/executions/<run-id>/production-reachability.json shows the check ran and its findings.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:2, area:checks, area:library, kind:check-impl, prevention</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-e34994e2" base-rev="eec3052eee409114c949529a7eafec84840b69be" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260503T123048-397071dc"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read those before editing. Ground decisions in what is already in the repository; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260503T123048-397071dc/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260503T123048-397071dc/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

If you write `.ddx/executions/20260503T123048-397071dc/no_changes_rationale.txt`, it MUST carry one of:

- `verification_command: <one-line shell command>` — the loop runs it (cwd = project root, 60s); exit 0 closes the bead, non-zero rejects.
- `status: needs_investigation` + `reason: <why>` — bead stays open for triage.

Bare rationales ("nothing to do") are rejected.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260503T123048-397071dc/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260503T123048-397071dc/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Bead overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and conservative project defaults (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) in this worktree — write whatever the bead asks for.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
Committed as `1605816f`.

**Summary**

Shipped `library/checks/go-production-reachability/` — a REACH-PROTO conforming check using `golang.org/x/tools/cmd/deadcode` for reachability. Self-tests (6) all pass: unwired blocks, wired passes, open-bead annotation passes, closed/missing bead rejected, test-only references don't satisfy.

Dogfooded via `.ddx/checks/production-reachability.yaml` pointing at `cli/`. Evidence at `.ddx/executions/20260503T123048-397071dc/production-reachability.json` shows the check ran (status=pass, no non-test Go files in this bead's diff).

Lefthook pre-commit passes. The pre-existing `TestReviewEvidenceApproveAttributesToTier` failure in `cli/cmd` was verified to fail on the base rev `eec3052e` and is unrelated to this bead.
